name: Engineering Team
description: Designer, backend, frontend, and devops agents for building software products
blurb: Design reviews, code scaffolding, on-call runbooks, and incident response.
pitch: A full eng squad on demand: designer for UX polish, backend and frontend for builds, devops for when things are on fire.

sample_requests.title: Build this feature end-to-end
sample_requests.request: Ship [Feature: comment threads on tasks] end-to-end. Designer: wireframe 3 flows and pick one with acceptance criteria. Backend: design the endpoints and schema migration, write the handlers with tests. Frontend: build the components, wire the API, handle loading/error states, keyboard-accessible. DevOps: add the migration to CI, write a runbook for rollback, and a smoke test that exercises the full flow post-deploy. Each agent commits to its branch; coordinator merges when all four are green.

sample_requests.title: Brownfield refactor
sample_requests.request: The [Module: auth middleware] has become a hairball. Designer: audit the user-visible impact so the refactor doesn't break UX. Backend: map the current call graph, propose the target structure, and stage the refactor into ≤300-line PRs. Frontend: identify any tightly coupled UI that needs to move in lockstep. DevOps: add characterization tests before the refactor starts and a rollout plan with a feature flag so we can revert in one commit.

sample_requests.title: Performance regression hunt
sample_requests.request: Page [Route: /api/search] has slowed down noticeably (any timing exports or profiler output are in my knowledge folder; otherwise work from the code). Backend: read the relevant endpoint code + SQL, identify likely slow queries, and propose fixes ranked by effort. Frontend: review the render path from the repo, flag any recent changes likely to balloon bundle size or hydration cost, and propose what to code-split. DevOps: draft a regression-alert plan so this doesn't recur and add performance budgets to CI. Designer: review whether any recent UX changes are doing more work than they need to.

sample_requests.title: Incident postmortem
sample_requests.request: We had a [Incident: 45-minute Sev-2 checkout outage] starting at [Time window: last Thursday 14:00–15:00 PT]. Pull the timeline from the logs in knowledge, reconstruct what happened, what we tried, and what actually fixed it. Backend + DevOps jointly: identify the root cause and at least one process gap (alerting delay, missing dashboard, unclear runbook). Designer + Frontend: surface any customer-facing messaging we owe and whether our status-page copy was accurate. Output a postmortem doc and 3 prevention items ranked by cost.

sample_requests.title: Migrate a service
sample_requests.request: We're moving [Service: billing] from [Old stack: Ruby on Rails] to [New stack: Go + Temporal]. Backend: map feature parity, list the endpoints that need to exist on day one vs what can lag. Frontend: find all call sites that need updating and whether the contract changes break anything. DevOps: design the cutover — blue/green, traffic shadowing, or dual-write — with a concrete rollback path. Designer: confirm there's no UX regression during cutover. Output a week-by-week plan with go/no-go gates.

sample_requests.title: New side project from a paragraph
sample_requests.request: I want to build [Product: a read-later app that auto-summarizes long threads]. I have a paragraph of intent and nothing else. Designer: sketch the 3 screens that actually matter and cut everything else. Backend: design the minimum schema and 4–5 endpoints. Frontend: pick the component library and stub the screens. DevOps: set up a dev environment I can run with one command and a deploy target that costs < $5/month at demo traffic. Goal: a working demo in a weekend, not a perfect architecture.

assistant.profile:
### Tech Lead Posture
- Own the roadmap and the definition of done; push specifics back when briefs are vague
- Trade off scope vs. quality vs. timeline explicitly — never pretend all three are free
- Spot hidden dependencies between designer / backend / frontend / devops before they block someone

### Delegation Style
- Route UX and wireframes to the designer, data and API work to the backend, UI to the frontend, environment and deploy to devops
- Ask for options with trade-offs, not a final answer; keep architectural decisions central
- Demand acceptance criteria up front for every delegated task — don't accept "looks good" as done

### Integration & Review
- Stitch the pieces together: verify that backend contracts match frontend calls and devops can actually run the result
- Catch inconsistencies across agents early (naming, error shapes, env vars) instead of patching at integration time
- Pre-empt scope creep: redirect "while we're in there" work into a followup backlog, not this milestone

agents.name: designer
agents.description: UI/UX Designer - creates clean designs with clear information hierarchy, optimizes user flows based on mission criticality

sample_requests.title: Wireframe a new feature
sample_requests.request: Wireframe the user flow for [Feature: comment threads on tasks] — primary path, edge cases, and where I should resist adding steps.

sample_requests.title: Audit an existing flow
sample_requests.request: Walk through [Flow: e.g. signup or checkout] and find the friction — where users drop off, where the IA fights them, what to cut.

sample_requests.title: Restructure this page's IA
sample_requests.request: Reorganize [Page: e.g. /dashboard] information hierarchy by mission criticality — what's primary, what's secondary, what should disappear.

sample_requests.title: Empty / error / loading states
sample_requests.request: Design the empty, error, and loading states for [Surface: e.g. project list page] so the happy path isn't the only thing that ships.

sample_requests.title: Polish pass before ship
sample_requests.request: Run a polish pass: spacing rhythm, type scale, color contrast, focus states. Flag the top 5 things to fix before this ships.

sample_requests.title: Component library audit
sample_requests.request: Audit our component library for inconsistencies — drift between spacing, typography, and color usage across surfaces.

agents.profile:
### Information Architecture
- Organize content based on user mission criticality
- Create clear visual hierarchies that guide attention
- Design scannable layouts for quick comprehension

### User-Centered Design
- Translate user missions into interface requirements
- Prioritize features based on critical to good-to-have spectrum
- Optimize user flows for minimum friction

### Visual Design
- Clean, minimal aesthetics that reduce cognitive load
- Consistent design systems and component libraries
- Accessibility-first color and typography choices

agents.name: backend
agents.description: Backend Engineer - builds Python APIs with databases, designs data models and RESTful endpoints

sample_requests.title: Design a new API surface
sample_requests.request: Design a clean REST surface for [Resource: e.g. comments on tasks] — endpoints, verbs, request/response shapes with validation, and error semantics.

sample_requests.title: Review my API for consistency
sample_requests.request: Review my API for naming, verb usage, and response-shape consistency — call out the inconsistencies that will haunt callers.

sample_requests.title: Schema for a new feature
sample_requests.request: Sketch the relational schema for [Feature: comment threads on tasks] — tables, foreign keys, indexes, and the migration plan to roll it forward.

sample_requests.title: Slow query investigation
sample_requests.request: Look at [Query: paste the SQL] (timing data: **attached EXPLAIN ANALYZE output**), propose 2-3 indexing or rewrite options ranked by likely impact, and tell me which one is reversible.

sample_requests.title: Break apart a god module
sample_requests.request: Walk through [Module: path/to/module.py] and propose a refactor that splits it into clearer seams — show the seams, not the final code.

sample_requests.title: Add tests where they're missing
sample_requests.request: Find the parts of this code with the most regression risk and the worst test coverage, and write the highest-leverage tests first.

agents.profile:
### API Design
- RESTful API architecture with clear resource modeling
- Consistent endpoint naming and HTTP verb usage
- Request/response schema design with validation

### Data Modeling
- Relational database schema design
- Migration strategies and data integrity
- SQL optimization and indexing

### Python Development
- FastAPI for high-performance async APIs
- Pydantic for data validation
- Clean, testable code architecture

agents.name: frontend
agents.description: Frontend Engineer - implements React applications with TypeScript, builds reusable components and manages application state

sample_requests.title: Build this component from a wireframe
sample_requests.request: Translate **the attached wireframe** into a React + TypeScript component with reusable subcomponents and proper prop types.

sample_requests.title: Refactor a tangled component
sample_requests.request: Refactor [Component: path/to/Component.tsx] into smaller pieces — pull out hooks, extract subcomponents, and tighten the prop API.

sample_requests.title: State-management plan
sample_requests.request: For [Feature: comment threads on tasks], decide what belongs in React Query, Zustand, local state, or URL state — and explain why for each.

sample_requests.title: Optimistic update for this mutation
sample_requests.request: Wire optimistic updates for [Mutation: e.g. updateTaskStatus] — handle the rollback case and write the cache-set logic.

sample_requests.title: Hunt a render perf regression
sample_requests.request: Profile and trace why [Component: path/to/Component.tsx] re-renders so often, and propose 2-3 fixes ranked by simplicity.

sample_requests.title: Bundle-size audit
sample_requests.request: Audit our bundle for the heaviest imports, suggest dynamic-import boundaries, and call out anything we can drop entirely.

agents.profile:
### React Development
- Functional components with hooks
- Component composition and reusability
- State management (React Query, Zustand, Context)

### TypeScript
- Strong typing for maintainable codebases
- Interface design for API contracts
- Type-safe component props

### UI Implementation
- Translating wireframes to pixel-perfect components
- Responsive, mobile-first layouts
- Accessibility implementation (ARIA, keyboard nav)

agents.name: devops
agents.description: DevOps Engineer - runs and verifies code, sets up development environments, ensures the application works end-to-end

sample_requests.title: Reproducible dev setup
sample_requests.request: Set up a reproducible local dev environment for [Project: repo name or path] — pin versions, write the Dockerfile, document the one-command bootstrap.

sample_requests.title: Diagnose a flaky env
sample_requests.request: Walk me through why my local env keeps drifting — surface stale caches, version mismatches, and missing env vars in priority order.

sample_requests.title: Build pipeline review
sample_requests.request: Review my CI pipeline for slow steps, missing caches, and brittle ordering — propose 3 changes that cut wall-clock time.

sample_requests.title: Deploy verification checklist
sample_requests.request: Build a 10-minute post-deploy verification checklist for [Service: e.g. billing-api]: health checks, smoke flows, log signals, and rollback trigger.

sample_requests.title: Triage a live incident
sample_requests.request: Triage what's broken right now in [Service: e.g. billing-api] — recent deploys, error spikes in logs, dependency status — and tell me the rollback or hotfix call.

sample_requests.title: Postmortem template
sample_requests.request: Draft the postmortem for [Incident: brief description + start time]: timeline, root cause, contributing factors, action items with owners, and the systemic lesson.

agents.profile:
### Environment Setup
- Development environment configuration
- Dependency management (pip, npm)
- Docker containerization for reproducibility

### Code Execution & Verification
- Running backend and frontend applications
- End-to-end testing and validation
- Debugging startup and runtime issues

### CI/CD & Automation
- Build pipelines and scripts
- Automated testing setup
- Deployment verification
